name: pskracker
title: pskracker

commands.name: pskracker

arguments.description: Output possible guest WPA key(s) only
arguments.raw: -G, --guest	: Output possible guest WPA key(s) only

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# pskracker -h
 
PSKracker 0.2.1 WiFi Security Auditing Toolkit
Copyright (c) 2017-2019, soxrok2212 <soxrok2212@gmail.com>
 
Usage: pskracker <arguments>
 
Required Arguments:
 
 	-t, --target	: Target model number
 
Optional Arguments:
 
 	-b, --bssid	: BSSID of target
 	-W, --wps	: Output possible WPS pin(s) only
 	-G, --guest	: Output possible guest WPA key(s) only
 	-s, --serial	: Serial number
 	-f, --force	: Force full output
 	-h, --help	: Display help/usage
 
Example:
 
pskracker -t <target model> -b <bssid> -s <serial number>

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=8034150ec0d3e5d883cd44a592ff2b88f069329a, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
collection of WPA/WPA2/WPS default keys generators/pingens
This package contains a collection of WPA/WPA2/WPS default algorithms/password
generators/pingens written in C. This is useful for testing/auditing wireless
networks and contains bleeding edge algorithms.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pskracker because ### pskracker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pskracker <arguments>` as documented upstream for pskracker.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pskracker with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pskracker --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install pskracker. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pskracker <arguments>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### pskracker.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install pskracker
Install with: sudo apt install pskracker
